title: Heatwave Alert for Tamil Nadu: Temperatures May Reach 106°F in May - LiveChennai
search_term: heatwave (Ariyalur OR Chennai OR Coimbatore OR Cuddalore OR Dharmapuri OR Dindigul OR Erode OR Kanchipuram OR Kanyakumari OR Karur OR Krishnagiri OR Madurai OR Nagapattinam OR Namakkal OR Nilgiris OR Perambalur OR Pudukkottai OR Ramanathapuram OR Salem OR Sivaganga OR Thanjavur OR Theni OR "Thoothukudi (Tuticorin)" OR Tiruchirappalli OR Tirunelveli OR Tiruppur OR Tiruvallur OR Tiruvannamalai OR Tiruvarur OR Vellore OR Viluppuram OR Virudhunagar) Tamil Nadu
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that summer temperatures across Tamil Nadu are set to rise sharply, with levels possibly touching 106°F (41°C). Heatwave conditions are expected in May.
Due to the delayed sea breeze, especially in Chennai and northern districts, daytime temperatures are likely to increase further. Recent data shows that April temperatures have consistently remained high, often crossing 102°F to 105°F in the past decade.
Meteorologists state that as the sun shifts northward, heat intensifies during May and June, marking the Agni Nakshatram (Kathiri Veyil) period, known for extreme heat.
From April 13 to 17, temperatures are expected to stay 2–3°C above normal, with humidity levels between 66% and 80%, increasing discomfort. Districts like Vellore and Karur have already recorded around 105°F, and Chennai may see further rise after April 15.
The IMD has also cautioned that Tamil Nadu may experience around 6 days of heatwave in May, which could affect normal life.
Residents are advised to stay hydrated, avoid peak heat hours, and take necessary precautions.
